00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: class resolver.c.
00029  * FILE:      classresolver.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for loading and resolving class definitions.
00031  *            These routines should not be depending upon the interpreter
00032  *            or the garbage collector.
00033  *=======================================================================*/
00034 
00035 /*=========================================================================
00036  * Include files
00037  *=======================================================================*/
00038 
00039 #include "wrappers.h"
00040 
00041 #include "oobj.h"
00042 #include "tree.h"
00043 #include "signature.h"
00044 #include "path.h"
00045 #include "sys_api.h"
00046 #include "convert_md.h"
00047 
00048 /*=========================================================================
00049  * Globals and extern declarations
00050  *=======================================================================*/
00051 
00052 ClassClass *classJavaLangObject;
00053 ClassClass *classJavaLangClass = 0;
00054 ClassClass *classJavaLangString;
00055 ClassClass *classJavaLangThrowable;
00056 ClassClass *classJavaLangError;
00057 ClassClass *classJavaLangException;
00058 ClassClass *classJavaLangRuntimeException;
00059 ClassClass *classJavaLangThreadDeath;
00060 
00061 ClassClass *interfaceJavaLangCloneable;
00062 ClassClass *interfaceJavaIoSerializable;
00063 
00064 bool inline_jsr_on = true;
00065 
00066 static bool RunClinit(ClassClass * cb);
00067 
00068 static ClassClass *InitializeAndResolveClass(ClassClass *cb, bool resolve);
00069 static char *Locked_InitializeClass(ClassClass * cb, char **detail);
00070 static char *Locked_LinkClass(ClassClass * cb, char **detail);
00071 char * LinkClass(ClassClass *cb, char **detail);
00072 static ClassClass *FindLoadedClass(char *name,
00073                    struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader);
00074 static ClassClass *Locked_FindArrayClassFromClass(struct execenv *ee,
00075                           char *name,
00076                           ClassClass *from);
00077 static void InitPrimitiveClasses();
00078 
00079 /* An Explanation of Class-related Locks
00080  *
00081  * There are two global locks related to class loading:
00082  *
00083  * LOADCLASS_LOCK: ensures that only one thread is loading a class at
00084  * a given time. This eliminates the possibility of two threads loading
00085  * classes with the same name and same loader.
00086  *
00087  * BINCLASS_LOCK: ensures that only one thread is updating the global
00088  * class table (binclasses). This lock is also grabbed by the GC to ensure
00089  * that the GC always sees a valid global class table state.
00090  *
00091  * In addition, each class may have its own associated locks that are
00092  * created with monitorEnter(). ResolveClass, for example, need to
00093  * first grab this lock to ensure that the class is resolved only by
00094  * one thread, rather than simultaneously by multiple threads.
00095  */
00096 
00097 sys_mon_t *_loadclass_lock;
00098 sys_mon_t *_binclass_lock;
00099 
00100 static struct fieldblock **
00101 addslots(struct fieldblock ** fb, ClassClass * cb)
00102 {
00103     long n = cbFieldsCount(cb);
00104     struct fieldblock *sfb = cbFields(cb);
00105     if (cbSuperclass(cb))
00106     fb = addslots(fb, cbSuperclass(cb));
00107     while (--n >= 0) {
00108     *fb++ = sfb;
00109     sfb++;
00110     }
00111     return fb;
00112 }
00113 
00114 int
00115 Locked_makeslottable(ClassClass * clb)
00116 {
00117     ClassClass *sclb;
00118     int         nslots = 0;
00119 
00120     if (cbSlotTable(clb)) {
00121     return SYS_OK;
00122     }
00123     sclb = clb;
00124     while (sclb) {
00125     long        n = cbFieldsCount(sclb);
00126     struct fieldblock *fb = cbFields(sclb);
00127     while (--n >= 0) {
00128         nslots++;
00129         fb++;
00130     }
00131     if (cbSuperclass(sclb) == 0) {
00132         break;
00133     }
00134     sclb = cbSuperclass(sclb);
00135     }
00136     cbSlotTableSize(clb) = nslots;
00137     if (nslots == 0) {
00138     nslots++;
00139     }
00140     cbSlotTable(clb) = (struct fieldblock **)
00141     sysMalloc(nslots * sizeof(struct fieldblock *));
00142     if (cbSlotTable(clb) == 0) {
00143     return SYS_NOMEM;
00144     }
00145     addslots(cbSlotTable(clb), clb);
00146     return SYS_OK;
00147 }
00148 
00149 int
00150 makeslottable(ClassClass * clb)
00151 {
00152     int result;
00153     LOADCLASS_LOCK();
00154     result = Locked_makeslottable(clb);
00155     LOADCLASS_UNLOCK();
00156     return result;
00157 }
00158 
00159 #if 0
00160 static char *
00161 copyclassname(char *src, char *dst)
00162 {
00163     sysAssert(*src == SIGNATURE_CLASS);
00164     src++;
00165     while (*src && *src != SIGNATURE_ENDCLASS)
00166     *dst++ = *src++;
00167     *dst = 0;
00168     return src;
00169 }
00170 #endif
00171 
00172 static char *
00173 ResolveFields(ClassClass *cb, unsigned slot)
00174 {
00175     struct fieldblock *fb;
00176     int size;
00177 
00178     fb = cbFields(cb);
00179     for (size = 0; size < (int) cbFieldsCount(cb); size++, fb++) {
00180     char *signature = fieldsig(fb);
00181     int size = (((signature[0] == SIGNATURE_LONG ||
00182               signature[0] == SIGNATURE_DOUBLE)) ? 2 : 1);
00183     fb->ID = NameAndTypeToHash(fb->name, signature);
00184     if (fb->access & ACC_STATIC) {
00185         /* Do nothing.  Handled when the class is loaded. */
00186     } else {
00187         fb->u.offset = slot;
00188         slot += size * sizeof(OBJECT);
00189     }
00190 #ifdef UNUSED
00191     if ((fb->access & (ACC_STATIC | ACC_TRANSIENT)) == 0) {
00192         for (s = fieldname(fb); (c = *s++) != 0;)
00193         thishash = thishash * 7 + c;
00194         for (s = fieldsig(fb); (c = *s++) != 0;)
00195         thishash = thishash * 7 + c;
00196     }
00197 #endif
00198     }
00199     if (slot > 65535) {
00200         return "java/lang/InternalError";
00201     }
00202 
00203     cbInstanceSize(cb) = slot;
00204 #ifdef UNUSED
00205     cbThisHash(cb) = thishash;
00206     if (cbSuperclass(cb))
00207     cbTotalHash(cb) = thishash - cbTotalHash(unhand(cbSuperclass(cb)));
00208     else
00209         cbTotalHash(cb) = thishash;
00210     if (cbTotalHash(cb) < N_TYPECODES)
00211     cbTotalHash(cb) += N_TYPECODES;
00212 #endif
00213     return NULL;
00214 }
00215 
00216 static char *
00217 ResolveMethods(ClassClass *cb)
00218 {
00219     struct methodblock *mb;
00220     int size;
00221     struct methodtable *new_table;
00222     struct methodblock **super_methods;
00223     int mslot, super_methods_count;
00224     void *ptr;
00225     static unsigned finalizerID = 0;
00226 
00227     if (finalizerID == 0)
00228     finalizerID = NameAndTypeToHash(FINALIZER_METHOD_NAME,
00229                     FINALIZER_METHOD_SIGNATURE);
00230     mb = cbMethods(cb);
00231     for (size = 0; size < (int) cbMethodsCount(cb); size++, mb++) {
00232     mb->fb.ID = NameAndTypeToHash(mb->fb.name, mb->fb.signature);
00233     mb->fb.u.offset = 0;
00234     mb->invoker = 0;
00235     }
00236 
00237     if (cbIsInterface(cb)) {
00238     /* We don't really need to built a method table for interfaces. */
00239     cbMethodTable(cb) = NULL;
00240     cbMethodTableSize(cb) = 0;
00241     mb = cbMethods(cb);
00242     /* Each method's offset is its index in the interface */
00243     for (size = 0; size < (int) cbMethodsCount(cb); size++, mb++) {
00244         mb->fb.u.offset = size;
00245     }
00246     return NULL;
00247     }
00248 
00249     if (cbSuperclass(cb) != NULL) {
00250     ClassClass *super = cbSuperclass(cb);
00251     mslot = cbMethodTableSize(super);
00252     super_methods = cbMethodTable(super)->methods;
00253         super_methods_count = cbMethodTableSize(super);
00254     /* Inherit one's parent's finalizer, if it has one */
00255     cbFinalizer(cb) = cbFinalizer(cbSuperclass(cb));
00256     } else {
00257     mslot = 1;
00258     super_methods = NULL;
00259     super_methods_count = 0;
00260     cbFinalizer(cb) = NULL;
00261     }
00262 
00263     mb = cbMethods(cb);
00264     for (size = 0; size < (int) cbMethodsCount(cb); size++, mb++) {
00265     unsigned long ID = mb->fb.ID;
00266     struct methodblock **super_methods_p;
00267     int count;
00268 
00269     if ((mb->fb.access & ACC_STATIC) || (mb->fb.access & ACC_PRIVATE))
00270         continue;
00271     if (strcmp(mb->fb.name, "<init>") == 0)
00272         continue;
00273 
00274     /* If this item has its own finalizer method, grab it */
00275     if (mb->fb.ID == finalizerID) {
00276         if (no_finalizers) {
00277         panic("finalize methods should not appear");
00278         }
00279         cbFinalizer(cb) = mb;
00280     }
00281 
00282     for (super_methods_p = super_methods, count = super_methods_count;
00283            count > 0;
00284            super_methods_p++, count--) {
00285         if ((*super_methods_p != NULL) && ((*super_methods_p)->fb.ID == ID)) {
00286           /* Private methods are not inherited outside of the class. */
00287             if ((*super_methods_p)->fb.access & ACC_PRIVATE)
00288           continue;
00289           /* Package-private methods are not inherited outside of the
00290          package. */
00291             if (((*super_methods_p)->fb.access & ACC_PROTECTED) ||
00292             ((*super_methods_p)->fb.access & ACC_PUBLIC) ||
00293             IsSameClassPackage((*super_methods_p)->fb.clazz, cb)) {
00294             mb->fb.u.offset = (*super_methods_p)->fb.u.offset;
00295             break;
00296         }
00297           /*
00298         jio_fprintf(stderr, "!!!%s.%s\n", cbName(cb), mb->fb.name);
00299         */
00300         }
00301     }
00302 
00303     if (mb->fb.u.offset == 0) {
00304         mb->fb.u.offset = mslot;
00305         mslot++;
00306     }
00307     }
00308 
00309     if (mslot > 65535) {
00310         return "java/lang/InternalError";
00311     }
00312 
00313     /*
00314      *    This should be the only place that method tables are
00315      *    allocated.  We allocate more than we need here and mask the
00316      *    resulting pointer because the methodtable pointer field in
00317      *    object handles is overloaded and sometimes hold array types
00318      *    and array lengths.  We must ensure that method table pointers
00319      *    are allocated on an appropriate boundary so we don't lose any
00320      *    address bits when we mask off the type portion of the pointer.
00321      */
00322     ptr = sysMalloc(sizeof(struct methodtable)
00323                + (mslot - 1)* sizeof(struct methodblock *)
00324                + FLAG_MASK);
00325     if (ptr == NULL) {
00326     CCSet(cb, Error);
00327     return JAVAPKG "OutOfMemoryError";
00328     }
00329     cbMethodTableMem(cb) = ptr;
00330     new_table = (struct methodtable *)((((long)ptr) + FLAG_MASK) & LENGTH_MASK);
00331     new_table->classdescriptor = cb;
00332     memset((char *)new_table->methods, 0, mslot * sizeof(struct methodblock *));
00333     if (super_methods)
00334     memcpy((char *) new_table->methods,
00335            (char *) super_methods,
00336            super_methods_count * sizeof(struct methodblock *));
00337     mb = cbMethods(cb);
00338     for (size = 0; size < (int) cbMethodsCount(cb); size++, mb++) {
00339     int offset = (int)mb->fb.u.offset;
00340     if (offset > 0) {
00341         sysAssert(offset < mslot);
00342         mt_slot(new_table, offset) = mb;
00343     }
00344     }
00345     cbMethodTable(cb) = new_table;
00346     cbMethodTableSize(cb) = mslot;
00347 
00348     return NULL;
00349 }
00350 
00351 static char *
00352 ResolveInterfaces(ClassClass *cb, char **detail)
00353 {
00354     const int ITEM_SIZE =  sizeof(cbIntfMethodTable(cb)->itable[0]);
00355     bool isInterface = cbIsInterface(cb);
00356     if (cbImplementsCount(cb) == 0 && !isInterface) {
00357     /* classes that don't implement their own interfaces can just inherit
00358      * their parents imethodtable. */
00359     if (cb == classJavaLangObject) {
00360         /* We can preinitialize the imethodtable of java.lang.Object */
00361         static struct imethodtable t = { 0 };
00362         cbIntfMethodTable(cb) = &t;
00363     } else {
00364         ClassClass *super = cbSuperclass(cb);
00365         cbIntfMethodTable(cb) = cbIntfMethodTable(super);
00366     }
00367     return NULL;
00368     } else {
00369     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00370     ClassClass *super = cbSuperclass(cb);
00371     unsigned char *mallocSpace, *mallocSpaceEnd;
00372     ClassClass *icb;    /* temporary */
00373     struct imethodtable *super_itable = cbIntfMethodTable(super);
00374     struct imethodtable *this_itable = NULL;
00375     int super_itable_count = super_itable->icount;
00376     int i, j, k, icount, mcount;
00377 
00378     /* Resolve all the interfaces that this class implements, and
00379      * make sure that they all really are interfaces
00380      *
00381      * icount will total all the interfaces that this class implements,
00382      *    (include interfaces implemented by our superclass, and by
00383      *    interfaces that we implement.)
00384      * mcount will total the total amount of space (in sizeof(long)) for
00385      *    which we'll have to allocate space for in the offsets table.
00386      */
00387     icount = super_itable_count + (isInterface ? 1 : 0);
00388     mcount = 0;
00389     for (i = 0; i < (int)(cbImplementsCount(cb)); i++) {
00390         int interface_index = cbImplements(cb)[i];
00391         struct imethodtable *sub_itable;
00392 
00393         icb = constant_pool[interface_index].clazz;
00394         if (!cbIsInterface(icb)) {
00395             *detail = "Implementing class";
00396         return JAVAPKG "IncompatibleClassChangeError";
00397         }
00398         sub_itable = cbIntfMethodTable(icb);
00399         icount += sub_itable->icount;
00400         if (!isInterface)
00401         for (j = sub_itable->icount; --j >= 0; )
00402             mcount += cbMethodsCount(sub_itable->itable[j].classdescriptor);
00403     }
00404 
00405     {
00406         int this_itable_size =
00407         offsetof(struct imethodtable, itable) + icount * ITEM_SIZE;
00408         int offsets_size = mcount * sizeof(unsigned long);
00409         mallocSpace = sysMalloc(this_itable_size + offsets_size);
00410         if (mallocSpace == NULL) {
00411         return JAVAPKG "OutOfMemoryError";
00412         }
00413         mallocSpaceEnd = mallocSpace + this_itable_size + offsets_size;
00414         this_itable = (struct imethodtable *)mallocSpace;
00415         mallocSpace += this_itable_size;
00416         sysAssert(mallocSpace <= mallocSpaceEnd);
00417     }
00418 
00419     cbIntfMethodTable(cb) = this_itable;
00420 
00421     /* Start filling in the table. */
00422     icount = 0;
00423     if (isInterface) {
00424         this_itable->itable[icount].classdescriptor = cb;
00425         this_itable->itable[icount].offsets = NULL;
00426         icount++;
00427     }
00428     if (super_itable_count > 0) {
00429         /* We can copy our superclass's offset table.  The offsets
00430            will stay the same.  */
00431         memcpy(&this_itable->itable[icount],
00432            &super_itable->itable[0],
00433            super_itable_count * ITEM_SIZE);
00434         icount += super_itable_count;
00435     }
00436     for (i = 0; i < (int)(cbImplementsCount(cb)); i++) {
00437         /* Add the interfaces that we implement, either directly, or
00438          * because those interfaces implement other interfaces.    */
00439         int interface_index = cbImplements(cb)[i];
00440         icb = constant_pool[interface_index].clazz;
00441         memcpy(&this_itable->itable[icount],
00442            &cbIntfMethodTable(icb)->itable[0],
00443            cbIntfMethodTable(icb)->icount * ITEM_SIZE);
00444         icount += cbIntfMethodTable(icb)->icount;
00445     }
00446     sysAssert(!isInterface || super_itable_count == 0);
00447     /* Delete duplicates from the table.  This is very rare, so it can
00448      * be quite inefficient.  */
00449     for (i = (isInterface ? 1 : super_itable_count); i < icount; i++) {
00450         icb = this_itable->itable[i].classdescriptor;
00451         for (j = 0; j < i; j++) {
00452         if (icb == this_itable->itable[j].classdescriptor) {
00453             /* We have an overlap.  Item i is a duplicate.  Delete from
00454              * the table */
00455             for (k = i + 1; k < icount; k++)
00456             this_itable->itable[k - 1] = this_itable->itable[k];
00457             icount--;
00458             i--;     /* Reconsider this entry! */
00459             break;
00460         }
00461         }
00462     }
00463     this_itable->icount = icount;
00464     if (isInterface || cbIsAbstract(cb)) {
00465         /* Nothing more to do for interfaces or abstract classes*/
00466         return NULL;
00467     }
00468 
00469     /* For each additional interface . . . */
00470     for (i = super_itable_count; i < icount; i++) {
00471         /* The table length is the number of interface methods */
00472         ClassClass *intfi = this_itable->itable[i].classdescriptor;
00473         int intfi_count = cbMethodsCount(intfi);
00474         unsigned long *offsets = (unsigned long *)mallocSpace;
00475         mallocSpace += intfi_count * sizeof(unsigned long);
00476         sysAssert(mallocSpace <= mallocSpaceEnd);
00477         this_itable->itable[i].offsets = offsets;
00478         /* Look at each interface method */
00479         for (j = 0; j < intfi_count; j++) {
00480         struct methodblock *imb = cbMethods(intfi) + j;
00481         if ((imb->fb.access & ACC_STATIC) != 0) {
00482             sysAssert(!strcmp(imb->fb.name, "<clinit>"));
00483             offsets[j] = 0;
00484         } else {
00485             /* Find the class method that implements the interface
00486              * method */
00487             unsigned ID = imb->fb.ID;
00488             struct methodblock **mbt = cbMethodTable(cb)->methods;
00489             for (k = cbMethodTableSize(cb) - 1; k >= 0; --k) {
00490             struct methodblock *mb = mbt[k];
00491             if (mb != NULL
00492                   && mb->fb.ID == ID && IsPublic(mb->fb.access)) {
00493                 offsets[j] = mb->fb.u.offset;
00494                 break;
00495             }
00496             }
00497                     /*
00498             if (k == -1) {
00499             *detail = "Unimplemented interface method";
00500             return JAVAPKG "IncompatibleClassChangeError";
00501             }
00502                     */
00503         }
00504         }
00505     }
00506     return NULL;
00507     }
00508 }
00509 
00510 char *
00511 InitializeClass(ClassClass * cb, char **detail)
00512 {
00513     char *result;
00514     monitorEnter(obj_monitor(cb));
00515     result = Locked_InitializeClass(cb, detail);
00516     monitorExit(obj_monitor(cb));
00517     return result;
00518 }
00519 
00520 char *
00521 ResolveClass(ClassClass *cb, char **detail)
00522 {
00523     char *result;
00524     if (CCIs(cb, Resolved))
00525         return 0;
00526     result = LinkClass(cb, detail);
00527     if (result == 0) {
00528         if (!RunClinit(cb)) {
00529         result = JAVAPKG "ExceptionInInitializerError";
00530         *detail = cbName(cb);
00531     }
00532     }
00533     return result;
00534 }
00535 
00536 char *
00537 LinkClass(ClassClass *cb, char **detail)
00538 {
00539     char *result;
00540     if (CCIs(cb, Linked))
00541         return 0;
00542     monitorEnter(obj_monitor(cb));
00543     result = Locked_LinkClass(cb, detail);
00544     monitorExit(obj_monitor(cb));
00545     return result;
00546 }
00547 
00548 /*
00549  * Detect class circularities from InitializeClass()
00550  */
00551 
00552 static void
00553 pushSeen(ExecEnv *ee, struct seenclass *seen)
00554 {
00555     struct seenclass *prev = ee->seenclasses.next;
00556     ee->seenclasses.next = seen;
00557     seen->next = prev;
00558 }
00559 
00560 static void
00561 popSeen(ExecEnv *ee, struct seenclass *seen)
00562 {
00563     if (seen != ee->seenclasses.next) /* paranoia */
00564     panic("popSeen: corrupt seen class stack");
00565     ee->seenclasses.next = ee->seenclasses.next->next;
00566 }
00567 
00568 static bool
00569 checkSeen(ExecEnv *ee, ClassClass *cb)
00570 {
00571     struct seenclass *seen = ee->seenclasses.next;
00572     while (seen) {
00573     if (cb == seen->cb)
00574         return true;
00575     seen = seen->next;
00576     }
00577     return false;
00578 }
00579 
00580 static char *
00581 Locked_InitializeClass(ClassClass * cb, char **detail)
00582 {
00583     ClassClass *super = 0;
00584     char *ret = 0;
00585     bool noLoader;
00586     int i;
00587     char buff[BUFSIZ];
00588     char *nativeName = &buff[0];
00589 
00590     if (CCIs(cb, Initialized))
00591     return NULL;
00592 
00593     noLoader = (cbLoader(cb) == 0);
00594     if (cbFieldsCount(cb) > 2000) {
00595     return JAVAPKG "ClassFormatError";
00596     }
00597     if ((strcmp(cbName(cb), CLS_RESLV_INIT_CLASS) == 0) && noLoader) {
00598     /* Temporarily disable class circularity checks */
00599     ExecEnv *ee = EE();
00600     struct seenclass *save = ee->seenclasses.next;
00601     ee->seenclasses.next = NULL;
00602     /* Note: don't bother restoring on (fatal) error during bootstrap */
00603 
00604     classJavaLangClass = cb;
00605     MakeClassSticky(cb);
00606     classJavaLangString =
00607         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "String", true);
00608     if (classJavaLangString == 0) {
00609         *detail = JAVAPKG "String";
00610         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00611     }
00612 /*    classJavaLangThreadDeath =
00613         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "ThreadDeath", TRUE);
00614     if (classJavaLangThreadDeath == 0) {
00615         *detail = JAVAPKG "ThreadDeath";
00616         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00617     }
00618     */
00619     classJavaLangThrowable =
00620         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "Throwable", true);
00621     if (classJavaLangThrowable == 0) {
00622         *detail = JAVAPKG "Throwable";
00623         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00624     }
00625     /*
00626     classJavaLangException =
00627         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "Exception", TRUE);
00628     if (classJavaLangException == 0) {
00629         *detail = JAVAPKG "Exception";
00630         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00631     }
00632     classJavaLangError =
00633                 FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "Error", TRUE);
00634     if (classJavaLangError == 0) {
00635         *detail = JAVAPKG "Error";
00636         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00637     }
00638     classJavaLangRuntimeException =
00639         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "RuntimeException", TRUE);
00640     if (classJavaLangRuntimeException == 0) {
00641         *detail = JAVAPKG "RuntimeException";
00642         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00643     }
00644     interfaceJavaLangCloneable =
00645         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, JAVAPKG "Cloneable", TRUE);
00646     if (interfaceJavaLangCloneable == 0) {
00647         *detail = JAVAPKG "Cloneable";
00648         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00649     }
00650     interfaceJavaIoSerializable =
00651         FindStickySystemClass(NULL, "java/io/Serializable", TRUE);
00652     if (interfaceJavaIoSerializable == 0) {
00653         *detail = "java/io/Serializable";
00654         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00655     }
00656 */
00657     /* Restore stack for class circularity checks */
00658     ee->seenclasses.next = save;
00659 
00660     } else if ((strcmp(cbName(cb), CLS_RESLV_INIT_OBJECT) == 0) && noLoader){
00661     classJavaLangObject = cb;
00662     MakeClassSticky(classJavaLangObject);
00663     }
00664 
00665     if (noLoader) {
00666     char *name = cbName(cb);
00667     if (strcmp(name, CLS_RESLV_INIT_REF) == 0) {
00668         CCSet(cb, SoftRef);
00669     }
00670     if (strncmp(name, "java/", 5) || strncmp(name, "sun/", 4)) {
00671         CCSet(cb, SysLock);
00672     }
00673     }
00674     if (cbSuperclass(cb) == NULL) {
00675     if (cbSuperName(cb)) {
00676         /* Check for class definition circularities */
00677         ExecEnv *ee = EE();
00678         struct seenclass this[1];
00679         if (checkSeen(ee, cb)) {
00680         *detail = cbName(cb);
00681         CCSet(cb, Error);
00682         return JAVAPKG "ClassCircularityError";
00683         }
00684         this->cb = cb;
00685         this->next = NULL;
00686         pushSeen(ee, this);
00687 
00688             /* Conversion for Japanese file names */
00689             utf2native(cbSuperName(cb), nativeName, BUFSIZ);
00690 
00691         super = FindClassFromClass(ee, nativeName, false, cb);
00692 
00693         popSeen(ee, this);
00694         if (super != NULL) {
00695         sysAssert(CCIs(super, Initialized));
00696         /* Don't allow a class to have a superclass it can't access */
00697         if (!VerifyClassAccess(cb, super, false)) {
00698             super = NULL;
00699         }
00700         }
00701         if (super != NULL) {
00702         cbSuperclass(cb) = cbHandle(super);
00703         if (CCIs(super, SoftRef))
00704             CCSet(cb, SoftRef);
00705         } else {
00706         ret = JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00707         *detail = cbSuperName(cb);
00708         cbSuperclass(cb) = NULL;
00709         CCSet(cb, Error);
00710         }
00711     } else if (cb == classJavaLangObject) {
00712         cbSuperclass(cb) = 0;
00713     } else {
00714         *detail = cbName(cb);
00715         return JAVAPKG "ClassFormatException";
00716     }
00717     }
00718 
00719     for (i = 0; i < (int)(cbImplementsCount(cb)); i++) {
00720         /* Be very careful, since not verified yet. . .
00721      */
00722         int interface_index = cbImplements(cb)[i];
00723     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00724     unsigned char *type_table =
00725         constant_pool[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00726     ClassClass *icb;
00727     int nconstants = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
00728     int iname_index;
00729     char *iname;
00730 
00731     if (interface_index <= 0 ||
00732         interface_index >= nconstants ||
00733         type_table[interface_index] != CONSTANT_Class ||
00734         !(iname_index = constant_pool[interface_index].i) ||
00735         iname_index <= 0 ||
00736         iname_index >= nconstants ||
00737         type_table[iname_index] !=
00738         (CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED)) {
00739         *detail = "Bad interface index";
00740         return JAVAPKG "ClassFormatError";
00741     }
00742 
00743     iname = constant_pool[iname_index].cp;
00744     if (iname == NULL || !IsLegalClassname(iname, false)) {
00745         *detail = "Bad interface name";
00746         return JAVAPKG "ClassFormatError";
00747     }
00748 
00749     {
00750         ExecEnv *ee = EE();
00751         struct seenclass this[1];
00752         if (checkSeen(ee, cb)) {
00753         *detail = cbName(cb);
00754         CCSet(cb, Error);
00755         return JAVAPKG "ClassCircularityError";
00756         }
00757         this->cb = cb;
00758         this->next = NULL;
00759         pushSeen(ee, this);
00760         icb = FindClassFromClass(ee, iname, false, cb);
00761         popSeen(ee, this);
00762         if (icb) {
00763             constant_pool[interface_index].clazz = icb;
00764         type_table[interface_index] |= CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED;
00765         } else {
00766             *detail = iname;
00767         CCSet(cb, Error);
00768         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00769         }
00770     }
00771     }
00772 
00773     CCSet(cb, Initialized);
00774 
00775     /* Make sure we know what classJavaLangClass is, and that it's method table
00776      * is filled in.
00777      */
00778     if (classJavaLangClass == 0) {
00779     classJavaLangClass =
00780           FindClassFromClass(0, CLS_RESLV_INIT_CLASS, true, cb);
00781     if (classJavaLangClass == 0) {
00782         return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00783     }
00784     }
00785 
00786     /* The following may not do anything useful if classJavaLangClass hasn't
00787      * been completely resolved.  But we clean up in ResolveClass
00788      */
00789 
00790     cbHandle(cb)->methods = cbMethodTable(classJavaLangClass);
00791     return ret;
00792 }
00793 
00794 static char *
00795 Locked_LinkClass(ClassClass * cb, char **detail)
00796 {
00797     ClassClass *super = 0;
00798     unsigned slot = 0;
00799     char *ret = 0;
00800     int i;
00801 
00802     if (CCIs(cb, Error)) {
00803     *detail = cbName(cb);
00804     return JAVAPKG "NoClassDefFoundError";
00805     }
00806 
00807     sysAssert(CCIs(cb, Initialized));
00808 
00809     if (CCIs(cb, Linked)) {
00810     return NULL;
00811     }
00812 
00813     if (cbSuperclass(cb)) {
00814     /* If this object has a superclass. . . */
00815     super = cbSuperclass(cb);
00816     if (!CCIs(super, Linked)) {
00817         if ((ret = LinkClass(super, detail)) != 0) {
00818         CCSet(cb, Error);
00819         return ret;
00820         }
00821     }
00822     sysAssert(CCIs(super, Linked));
00823     slot = cbInstanceSize(super);
00824     }
00825 
00826     for (i = 0; i < (int)(cbImplementsCount(cb)); i++) {
00827         int interface_index = cbImplements(cb)[i];
00828     cp_item_type *constant_pool = cbConstantPool(cb);
00829     ClassClass *icb;
00830     icb = constant_pool[interface_index].clazz;
00831     if (!CCIs(icb, Linked)) {
00832         if ((ret = LinkClass(icb, detail)) != 0) {
00833         CCSet(cb, Error);
00834         return ret;
00835         }
00836     }
00837     }
00838 
00839     sysAssert(!CCIs(cb, Error));
00840     sysAssert(!CCIs(cb, Linked));
00841 
00842     cbInstanceSize(cb) = -1;
00843     if ((ret = ResolveFields(cb, slot))) { /* May return "InternalError" */
00844         *detail = cbName(cb);
00845     CCSet(cb, Error);
00846     return ret;
00847     }
00848 
00849     if ((ret = ResolveMethods(cb))) {
00850         /* May return "OutOfMemoryError" or "InternalError" */
00851     *detail = cbName(cb);
00852     CCSet(cb, Error);
00853     return ret;
00854     }
00855 
00856     if ((ret = ResolveInterfaces(cb, detail))) { /* All sorts of errors */
00857     CCSet(cb, Error);
00858     return ret;
00859     }
00860 
00861     InitializeInvoker(cb);
00862 
00863     if ((verifyclasses == VERIFY_ALL) ||
00864         ((verifyclasses == VERIFY_REMOTE) && (cbLoader(cb) != NULL))) {
00865         if (!VerifyClass(cb)) {
00866             *detail = "";
00867             return JAVAPKG "VerifyError";
00868         }
00869     }
00870 
00871     CCSet(cb, Linked);
00872 
00873     /* We need this for bootstrapping.  We can't set the Handle's class block
00874      * pointer in Object or Class until Class has been resolved.
00875      */
00876     if (cb == classJavaLangClass) {
00877     int         j;
00878     ClassClass **pcb;
00879     BINCLASS_LOCK();
00880     for (j = nbinclasses, pcb = binclasses; --j >= 0; pcb++) {
00881         cbHandle(*pcb)->methods = cbMethodTable(cb);
00882     }
00883     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00884     InitPrimitiveClasses();
00885     }
00886     return NULL;
00887 }
00888 
00889 static bool
00890 RunClinit(ClassClass * cb)
00891 {
00892 
00893     if (CCIs(cb, Resolved))
00894         return true;
00895 
00896     if ((cbName(cb)[0] != SIGNATURE_ARRAY) && !cbIsPrimitive(cb)) {
00897     /* Don't need to initialize or verify array or primitive classes */
00898         return RunStaticInitializers(cb);
00899     } else if (cbName(cb)[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
00900     ClassClass *inner_cb =
00901         cbConstantPool(cb)[CONSTANT_POOL_ARRAY_CLASS_INDEX].clazz;
00902     if (inner_cb) {
00903         char *detail = 0;
00904         char *ret = ResolveClass(inner_cb, &detail);
00905         if (ret != NULL) {
00906             if (!exceptionOccurred(EE())) {
00907             SignalError(0, ret, detail);
00908         }
00909         CCSet(cb, Error);
00910         return false;
00911         } else {
00912             CCSet(cb, Resolved);
00913         }
00914     } else {
00915         CCSet(cb, Resolved);
00916     }
00917     } else {
00918         CCSet(cb, Resolved);
00919     }
00920     return true;
00921 }
00922 
00923 /* Find an already loaded class with the given name.  If no such class exists
00924  * then return 0.
00925  */
00926 
00927 #ifdef DEBUG
00928 void
00929 PrintLoadedClasses()
00930 {
00931     int         j;
00932     ClassClass **pcb, *cb;
00933     BINCLASS_LOCK();
00934     pcb = binclasses;
00935     for (j = nbinclasses; --j >= 0; pcb++) {
00936     cb = *pcb;
00937     jio_fprintf(stdout, "class=%s, 0x%x\n", cbName(cb), cbLoader(cb));
00938     }
00939     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00940 }
00941 #endif
00942 
00943 static ClassClass *
00944 FindLoadedClass(char *name, struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader)
00945 {
00946     int left, right, middle, result;
00947     ClassClass *cb = NULL;
00948 
00949     BINCLASS_LOCK();
00950     left = 0;
00951     right = nbinclasses - 1;
00952     result = 1;
00953     while (left <= right) {
00954         middle = (left + right)/2;
00955     cb = binclasses[middle];
00956     result = strcmp(name, cbName(cb));
00957     if (result == 0) {
00958         if (loader < cbLoader(cb)) {
00959             result = -1;
00960         } else if (loader > cbLoader(cb)) {
00961             result = 1;
00962         } else {
00963             result = 0;
00964         }
00965     }
00966     if (result < 0) {
00967         right = middle - 1;
00968     } else if (result > 0) {
00969         left = middle + 1;
00970     } else {
00971         break;
00972     }
00973     }
00974 
00975     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
00976     if (result == 0) {
00977         return cb;
00978     }
00979     return NULL;
00980 }
00981 
00982 /* We attempt to resolve it, also, if the "resolve" argument is true.
00983  * Otherwise, we only perform a minimal initialization on it, such that
00984  * it points to its superclass, which points to its superclass. . . .
00985  */
00986 
00987 static ClassClass *
00988 InitializeAndResolveClass(ClassClass *cb, bool resolve)
00989 {
00990     char *err, *detail = NULL;
00991     if ((err = InitializeClass(cb, &detail))) {
00992         /* Check for underlying error already posted */
00993     if (!exceptionOccurred(EE()))
00994         SignalError(0, err, detail);
00995     return 0;
00996     }
00997     if (resolve) {
00998         err = ResolveClass(cb, &detail);
00999     if (err) {
01000         /* Check for underlying error already posted */
01001         if (!exceptionOccurred(EE()))
01002             SignalError(0, err, detail);
01003         return 0;
01004     }
01005     }
01006     return cb;
01007 }
01008 
01009 /* Find the class with the given name.
01010  * If "resolve" is true, then we completely resolve the class.  Otherwise,
01011  * we only make sure that it points to its superclass, which points to its
01012  * superclass, . . . .
01013  */
01014 ClassClass *(*class_loader_func)(HObject *loader, char *name, long resolve) = 0;
01015 
01016 ClassClass *
01017 FindClass(struct execenv *ee, char *name, bool resolve)
01018 {
01019     return FindClassFromClass(ee, name, resolve, 0);
01020 }
01021 
01022 /*
01023  * lock_classes and unlock_classes are exported by the JIT interface,
01024  * but no longer used anywhere else. It grabs both locks to ensure
01025  * backward compatibility with 1.0.2.
01026  */
01027 void
01028 lock_classes()
01029 {
01030     LOADCLASS_LOCK();
01031     BINCLASS_LOCK();
01032 }
01033 
01034 void
01035 unlock_classes()
01036 {
01037     BINCLASS_UNLOCK();
01038     LOADCLASS_UNLOCK();
01039 }
01040 
01041 ClassClass *
01042 FindClassFromClass(struct execenv *ee, char *name, bool resolve,
01043            ClassClass *from)
01044 {
01045     ClassClass *cb = 0;
01046 
01047     if (name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
01048     cb = Locked_FindArrayClassFromClass(ee, name, from) ;
01049     if (cb && !exceptionOccurred(ee ? ee : EE()))
01050         return InitializeAndResolveClass(cb, resolve);
01051         return NULL;
01052     } else if (from != NULL && cbLoader(from)) {
01053     /* unlock the classes while the class is located by
01054      * the class loader. The class loader should do this
01055      * in a synchronized method
01056      */
01057     return ClassLoaderFindClass(ee, cbLoader(from), name, resolve);
01058     } else {
01059         extern char *current_class_name;
01060         ClassClass *result = NULL;
01061         char *old_class_name = current_class_name;
01062 
01063         current_class_name = name;
01064 
01065         LOADCLASS_LOCK();
01066     cb = FindLoadedClass(name, 0);
01067     if (cb == 0) {
01068         /* Check if there was an error; bail if so */
01069         if (ee == NULL)
01070         ee = EE();
01071         if (!exceptionOccurred(ee))
01072         cb = LoadClassLocally(name);
01073     }
01074     LOADCLASS_UNLOCK();
01075     if (cb && !exceptionOccurred(ee ? ee : EE())) {
01076         result = InitializeAndResolveClass(cb, resolve);
01077         }
01078         current_class_name = old_class_name;
01079         return result;
01080     }
01081 }
01082 
01083 /*
01084  * FindStickySystemClass is a variant of FindClassFromClass that makes the
01085  * class sticky (immune to class GC) if the class can be found.  It is only
01086  * used on system classes, i.e. classes with no class loader, so it's
01087  * equivalent to FindClassFromClass with "from" argument 0.  It returns 0
01088  * if the class can't be found, and like FindClass assumes that the caller
01089  * will deal with that.  Note that until the class has been made sticky it
01090  * must be kept somewhere where GC will find it.
01091  */
01092 ClassClass *
01093 FindStickySystemClass(struct execenv *ee, char *name, bool resolve)
01094 {
01095     ClassClass *cb;
01096     if ((cb = FindClassFromClass(ee, name, resolve, 0)) != NULL) {
01097     MakeClassSticky(cb);
01098     }
01099     return cb;
01100 }
01101 
01102 /* Find the array class with the specified name.  */
01103 static ClassClass *
01104 Locked_FindArrayClassFromClass(struct execenv *ee, char *name,
01105                    ClassClass *from) {
01106     struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *fromLoader =
01107             (from  == NULL) ? NULL : cbLoader(from);
01108     char *name_p;
01109     int depth, base_type;
01110     ClassClass *cb;
01111     struct Hjava_lang_ClassLoader *loader;
01112     ClassClass *inner_cb;
01113 
01114     sysAssert(name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY);
01115 
01116     if (fromLoader == NULL) {
01117     /* quick optimization if we know that we don't need a class loader */
01118     cb = FindLoadedClass(name, NULL);
01119     if (cb != NULL)
01120         return cb;
01121     }
01122 
01123 
01124     /* Strip off all the initial SIGNATURE_ARRAY's to determine the depth,
01125      * and also what's left.  When we're done, name_p points at the first
01126      * non-character, and depth is the count of the array depth
01127      */
01128     for (name_p = name, depth = 0; *name_p == SIGNATURE_ARRAY;
01129      name_p++, depth++);
01130 
01131     /* Look at the character to determine what type of array we have. */
01132     switch(*name_p++) {
01133         case SIGNATURE_INT:    base_type = T_INT; break;
01134     case SIGNATURE_LONG:   base_type = T_LONG; break;
01135     case SIGNATURE_FLOAT:  base_type = T_FLOAT; break;
01136     case SIGNATURE_DOUBLE: base_type = T_DOUBLE; break;
01137     case SIGNATURE_BOOLEAN:base_type = T_BOOLEAN; break;
01138     case SIGNATURE_BYTE:   base_type = T_BYTE; break;
01139     case SIGNATURE_CHAR:   base_type = T_CHAR; break;
01140     case SIGNATURE_SHORT:  base_type = T_SHORT; break;
01141     case SIGNATURE_CLASS:  base_type = T_CLASS; break;
01142         default:           return NULL;    /* bad signature */
01143     }
01144     if (base_type == T_CLASS) {
01145     char buffer_space[50];
01146     char *buffer = buffer_space;
01147     char *endChar = strchr(name_p, SIGNATURE_ENDCLASS);
01148     int nameLength = endChar - name_p;
01149     if (endChar == NULL || endChar[1] != '\0')
01150         return NULL;
01151     if (nameLength >= sizeof(buffer_space)) /* need bigger buffer */
01152         buffer = sysMalloc(nameLength + 1);
01153     memcpy(buffer, name_p, nameLength);
01154     buffer[nameLength] = '\0';
01155     inner_cb = FindClassFromClass(ee, buffer, false, from);
01156     if (buffer != buffer_space) /* free buffer, if we malloc'ed it */
01157         sysFree(buffer);
01158     if (inner_cb == NULL)
01159         return NULL;
01160     /* loader should either be fromLoader or NULL. */
01161     loader = cbLoader(inner_cb);
01162     } else {
01163     if (*name_p != '\0')            /* bad signature */
01164         return NULL;
01165     inner_cb = NULL;
01166     loader = NULL;
01167     }
01168 
01169     LOADCLASS_LOCK();
01170     cb = FindLoadedClass(name, loader);
01171     if (cb == NULL) {
01172     /* Create the fake array class corresponding to this.
01173      */
01174     cb = createFakeArrayClass(name, base_type, depth, inner_cb, loader);
01175     }
01176     LOADCLASS_UNLOCK();
01177     return cb;
01178 }
01179 
01180 /*
01181  * Convert a name and type to a hash code
01182  *
01183  * We make copies of all strings that go into the hash table
01184  * in case the class that is the source of the strings being
01185  * inserted is eventually unloaded.
01186  */
01187 unsigned NameAndTypeToHash(char *name, char *type)
01188 {
01189     extern struct StrIDhash *nameTypeHash;
01190 
01191     unsigned name_key;
01192     unsigned type_key;
01193     unsigned ret;
01194 
01195     NAMETYPEHASH_LOCK();
01196     if (((name_key = Str2ID(&nameTypeHash, name, 0, true)) == 0) ||
01197     ((type_key = Str2ID(&nameTypeHash, type, 0, true)) == 0)) {
01198     SignalError(0, JAVAPKG "OutOfMemoryError", 0);
01199     ret = 0;
01200     } else {
01201     ret = (name_key << 16) + type_key;
01202     }
01203     NAMETYPEHASH_UNLOCK();
01204 
01205     return ret;
01206 }
01207 
01208 /*
01209  * Pseudo-classes to represent primitive Java types.
01210  */
01211 
01212 ClassClass *class_void;
01213 ClassClass *class_boolean;
01214 ClassClass *class_byte;
01215 ClassClass *class_char;
01216 ClassClass *class_short;
01217 ClassClass *class_int;
01218 ClassClass *class_long;
01219 ClassClass *class_float;
01220 ClassClass *class_double;
01221 
01222 struct primtype {
01223     char        *name;
01224     char        typesig;
01225     unsigned char    typecode;
01226     unsigned char    slotsize;
01227     unsigned char    elementsize;
01228     ClassClass        **cellp;
01229 } const primitive_types[] = {
01230     { "void",    SIGNATURE_VOID,       T_VOID,    0, 0,    &class_void },
01231     { "boolean",SIGNATURE_BOOLEAN, T_BOOLEAN,    4, 1,    &class_boolean },
01232     { "byte",    SIGNATURE_BYTE,       T_BYTE,    4, 1,    &class_byte },
01233     { "char",    SIGNATURE_CHAR,       T_CHAR,    4, 2,    &class_char },
01234     { "short",    SIGNATURE_SHORT,   T_SHORT,    4, 2,    &class_short },
01235     { "int",    SIGNATURE_INT,       T_INT,    4, 4,    &class_int },
01236     { "long",    SIGNATURE_LONG,       T_LONG,    8, 8,    &class_long },
01237     { "float",    SIGNATURE_FLOAT,   T_FLOAT,    4, 4,    &class_float },
01238     { "double",    SIGNATURE_DOUBLE,  T_DOUBLE,    8, 8,    &class_double }
01239 };
01240 
01241 #define    PRIMITIVE_TYPE_COUNT    \
01242     (sizeof (primitive_types) / sizeof (primitive_types[0]))
01243 
01244 #define    GET_PRIMITIVE_CLASS(nm) \
01245     (class_##nm ? class_##nm : FindPrimitiveClass(#nm))
01246 
01247 ClassClass *
01248 FindPrimitiveClass(char *name)
01249 {
01250     int i;
01251     ClassClass *cb;
01252     const struct primtype *p;
01253 
01254     for (i = 0; i < PRIMITIVE_TYPE_COUNT; i++) {
01255     p = &primitive_types[i];
01256     if (strcmp(name, p->name) == 0) {
01257         cb = *p->cellp;
01258         if (cb == NULL) {
01259         char *err, *detail = NULL;
01260         cb = createPrimitiveClass(p->name, p->typesig, p->typecode,
01261             p->slotsize, p->elementsize);
01262         sysAssert(cb != NULL);
01263         if ((err = InitializeClass(cb, &detail)) != NULL)
01264             return NULL; /* IMPL_NOTE */
01265         if ((err = ResolveClass(cb, &detail)) != NULL)
01266             return NULL; /* IMPL_NOTE*/
01267         *p->cellp = cb;
01268         }
01269         return cb;
01270     }
01271     }
01272     return NULL;
01273 }
01274 
01275 static void
01276 InitPrimitiveClasses()
01277 {
01278     int i;
01279     for (i = 0; i < PRIMITIVE_TYPE_COUNT; i++)
01280     FindPrimitiveClass(primitive_types[i].name);
01281 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
